Profile.title: Profile
Profile.description: Represents a single DevFolio user profile page, acting as the central hub for all displayed information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Profile entity.

ownerId.description: Identifier for the user who owns/manages this profile, from an external authentication system (e.g., Firebase Auth). (Relationship: External User 1:N Profile)

githubUsername.description: The GitHub username used to fetch dynamic profile data and repositories.

displayName.description: The name to be displayed on the DevFolio profile, potentially overriding the GitHub name.

tagline.description: A short, engaging phrase or slogan displayed prominently on the profile.

aboutMe.description: A personalized introduction or biography for the 'About Me' section.

email.description: A contact email address for the profile owner.

websiteUrl.description: A link to the profile owner's personal website or portfolio.

resumeUrl.description: An optional link to the profile owner's resume or CV.

socialLinkIds.description: References to SocialLink entities associated with this profile. (Relationship: Profile 1:N SocialLink)

themeSettings.description: An array of theme setting objects for the profile.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the profile was last updated.

logoImageUrl.description: URL for the main logo image.

profilePhotoUrl.description: URL for the user's profile photo.

socialSharePhotoUrl.description: URL for the image used in social media link previews.

metaDescription.description: SEO meta description for the site.

SocialLink.title: SocialLink
SocialLink.description: Represents a single social media or external link to be displayed on a user's DevFolio profile.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SocialLink entity.

profileId.description: Reference to the Profile this social link belongs to. (Relationship: Profile 1:N SocialLink)

platform.description: The platform or type of the social link (e.g., 'linkedin', 'twitter', 'github', 'custom').

url.description: The URL of the social media profile or external link.

label.description: An optional custom label to display for the link, especially useful for 'custom' platforms.

displayOrder.description: A numeric value indicating the order in which this link should be displayed on the profile.

WorkExperience.title: WorkExperience
WorkExperience.description: Represents a single entry of professional work history for a user's DevFolio profile.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WorkExperience entity.

profileId.description: Reference to the Profile this work experience belongs to. (Relationship: Profile 1:N WorkExperience)

ownerId.description: Identifier for the user who owns/manages this profile, denormalized for security rules.

companyName.description: The name of the company where the experience was gained.

role.description: The job title or role held at the company.

location.description: The physical location of the company or role (e.g., 'San Francisco, CA' or 'Remote').

startDate.description: The start date of the work experience.

endDate.description: The end date of the work experience. Null if 'isCurrent' is true.

isCurrent.description: A boolean indicating if this is the user's current work position.

descriptionPoints.description: An array of strings, each representing a key responsibility or achievement in this role.

displayOrder.description: A numeric value indicating the order in which this experience should be displayed.

Blog.title: Blog
Blog.description: Represents a single blog post or article in the portfolio.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Blog entity.

profileId.description: Reference to the Profile this blog post belongs to.

ownerId.description: Identifier for the user who owns/manages this profile, denormalized for security rules.

slug.description: URL-friendly slug for the blog post.

title.description: The title of the blog post.

description.description: A short summary of the blog post.

date.description: The publication date of the blog post.

cover_image.description: URL for the blog post's cover image.

content.description: The full content of the blog post in Markdown format.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Education entity.

profileId.description: Reference to the Profile this education belongs to.

ownerId.description: Identifier for the user who owns/manages this profile, denormalized for security rules.

institution.description: The name of the educational institution.

from.description: The start year of the education.

to.description: The end year of the education.

Skill.title: Skill
Skill.description: Represents a single technology or skill in the tech stack.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Skill entity.

profileId.description: Reference to the Profile this skill belongs to.

ownerId.description: Identifier for the user who owns/manages this profile, from an external authentication system (e.g., Firebase Auth).

displayOrder.description: A numeric value indicating the order in which this skill should be displayed.

Comment.title: Comment
Comment.description: Represents a single comment on a blog post.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Documents must include an 'ownerId' field corresponding to the Firebase Auth UID of the user who owns this profile. Profiles are publicly readable, but only the 'ownerId' can create, update, or delete them.

params.name: profileId
params.description: Unique identifier for the user's DevFolio profile.

definition.description: Stores social media and external links associated with a specific user profile. Each document includes 'profileId' to link to its parent and a denormalized 'ownerId' from the parent Profile for authorization independence. These links are publicly readable but editable only by the profile's 'ownerId'.

params.name: socialLinkId
params.description: Unique identifier for the social link.

definition.description: Stores professional work history entries for a specific user profile. Each document includes 'profileId' to link to its parent and a denormalized 'ownerId' from the parent Profile for authorization independence. These entries are publicly readable but editable only by the profile's 'ownerId'.

params.name: workExperienceId
params.description: Unique identifier for the work experience entry.

definition.description: Stores blog posts for a specific user profile. Each document includes 'profileId' to link to its parent and a denormalized 'ownerId' from the parent Profile for authorization independence. These are publicly readable but editable only by the profile's 'ownerId'. This collection contains a 'comments' subcollection.

params.name: blogId
params.description: Unique identifier for the blog post.

definition.description: Stores comments on a specific blog post. Publicly readable, and creatable by anyone. Deletable only by the profile owner.

params.name: blogId
params.description: Identifier of the parent Blog Post.

definition.description: Stores education entries for a specific user profile. Each document includes 'profileId' to link to its parent and a denormalized 'ownerId' from the parent Profile for authorization independence. These are publicly readable but editable only by the profile's 'ownerId'.

params.name: educationId
params.description: Unique identifier for the education entry.

definition.description: Stores tech stack skills for a specific user profile. Each document includes 'profileId' to link to its parent and a denormalized 'ownerId' from the parent Profile for authorization independence. These are publicly readable but editable only by the profile's 'ownerId'.

Stores portfolio projects for a specific user profile. Each document includes 'profileId' to link to its parent and a denormalized 'ownerId' from the parent Profile for authorization independence. These are publicly readable but editable only by the profile's 'ownerId'.
Stores project orders. Readable only by the profile owner, but creatable by anyone.

A collection used for Database Access Control (DBAC) to define administrative roles. The existence of a document at `/admin_users/{uid}` grants administrative privileges to the user with the corresponding UID.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of a user who holds an administrative role.

firestore.reasoning:
This Firestore structure is designed following the core principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and consistent Access Modeling to ensure security, scalability, and debuggability. 

**Authorization Independence:** The central strategy for achieving authorization independence (eliminating `get()` calls in security rules) is through denormalization. Each `Profile` document at `/profiles/{profileId}` stores an `ownerId` field, which corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID of the user who owns that profile. For all subcollections (`socialLinks`, `workExperiences`, `blogs`, `educations`), each document will *also* contain the `ownerId` denormalized from its parent `Profile`. This ensures that security rules for creating, updating, or deleting any subcollection document can directly check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.ownerId` without needing to perform a `get()` operation on the parent `Profile` document. This allows for atomic operations and simplifies rule logic considerably.

**Structural Segregation & QAPs:** All user profiles and their associated sub-data (social links, work experience, publications) are designed to be publicly readable but privately editable by their respective `ownerId`. This means that all documents within `/profiles/{profileId}` and its subcollections (`/profiles/{profileId}/socialLinks`, etc.) share a homogeneous security posture regarding read access (public). This directly supports the Query as Permissions (QAP) principle: since all data in these collections is intended for public consumption, a `list` query will naturally return all documents, and no filtering is performed by security rules for read access. Write access is strictly controlled by the denormalized `ownerId`, ensuring only the profile owner can modify their data.

**Access Modeling:**
*   **User-Owned Data:** The primary data, `Profile` and its related sub-entities, is structured hierarchically (`/profiles/{profileId}/subcollection/{subDocId}`). This clearly defines ownership and relationships. The `ownerId` field within each document (`Profile`, `SocialLink`, `WorkExperience`, `Blog`, `Education`) links it to the Firebase Auth `uid`.
*   **Global Roles (DBAC):** For the administrative dashboard login requirement, a dedicated collection `/admin_users/{userId}` is introduced. The mere existence of a document for a given `userId` in this collection grants administrative privileges. This 'existence over content' approach for roles is simple to implement and highly debuggable in security rules (`exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/admin_users/$(request.auth.uid))`).

**Overall:** This structure prioritizes explicit authorization logic within each document, simplifying security rules and making them easier to test and maintain. The clear separation of public read access from owner-only write access, coupled with the hierarchical pathing, ensures a robust and predictable data model for DevFolio.
